# AMO Toolkit — Full Content

**Live site:** https://amotoolkit.com/
**Author:** Saumitra Phatak — Physics PhD candidate, Purdue University, Hood Lab (ultracold atoms / optical tweezers)
**Source:** https://github.com/saumitraphatak/amo-career
**Purpose:** Career and research toolkit for Atomic, Molecular & Optical (AMO) physicists. Contains 22+ interactive physics tools/guides across 27 pages, a quantum computing industry landscape, 14 quantum fundamentals topics, career/literature reference pages, and experimental technique guides. Built with pure HTML/CSS/Vanilla JS — no build tools, KaTeX for math rendering, Chart.js for interactive plots.

---

## SITE STRUCTURE

```
home.html               — Landing page (tool grid, learn section, about)

```

## TOOL 1: ATOM LIBRARY
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/atom-library.html
**Accent color:** #38bdf8 (sky blue)

### Overview
Reference table of 15 laser-coolable atomic species organized in 4 family tabs. Aimed at AMO experimenters choosing a species or needing spectroscopic constants for calculation.

### Atom Families
- **Alkali metals:** Li-6, Li-7, Na-23, K-39, K-40, K-41, Rb-85, Rb-87, Cs-133
- **Alkaline-earth-like:** Sr-88, Sr-87, Yb-171, Yb-174
- **Other:** Ca-40, He-4* (metastable)

### Data per atom
| Property | Description |
|---|---|
| Mass (u) | Atomic mass units |
| D1 wavelength (nm) | S₁/₂ → P₁/₂ transition |
| D2 wavelength (nm) | S₁/₂ → P₃/₂ transition (main cooling transition) |
| Natural linewidth Γ/2π (MHz) | Inverse of excited state lifetime |
| Saturation intensity I_sat (mW/cm²) | I_sat = πhcΓ/3λ³ |
| Recoil scale 2E_rec/k_B (μK) | Two-recoil absorption-plus-emission heating scale; single recoil is E_rec/k_B = ℏ²k²/(2mk_B) |
| Doppler temperature T_D (μK) | T_D = ℏΓ/(2k_B) |
| Nuclear spin I | Relevant for hyperfine structure and Fermi statistics |

### Key Rb-87 constants (most common species)
- Mass: 86.909 u
- D2 wavelength: 780.241 nm
- Linewidth: Γ/2π = 6.065 MHz
- I_sat = 1.67 mW/cm²
- T_Doppler = 145 μK
- 2E_recoil/k_B = 0.362 μK; single recoil E_recoil/k_B = 0.181 μK
- Nuclear spin I = 3/2 → Bosonic

### Key Cs-133 constants
- Mass: 132.905 u, D2: 852.347 nm, Γ/2π = 5.234 MHz
- I_sat = 1.10 mW/cm², T_D = 125 μK, 2E_recoil/k_B = 0.198 μK

### US Research Groups (selected)
Rb: Greiner (Harvard), Lukin (Harvard), Kaufman (Harvard), Vuletic (MIT), Thompson (Caltech)
Cs: Chin (Chicago), DeMille (Chicago), Ye (JILA)
Sr: Ye (JILA), Kaubruegger (Innsbruck), Norcia (NIST)
Yb: Pagano (Maryland), Lukin (Harvard-Yb), Scazza (Florence)

---

## TOOL 2: LAB TECHNIQUES
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/lab-techniques.html
**Accent color:** #34d399 (emerald)

### 8 Experimental Techniques (accordion sections)

**1. Laser Cooling (Doppler)**
- Force: F = ℏk·Γ·s/[2(1+s+(2Δ/Γ)²)] − (opposite beam)
- Damping coefficient α = −8ℏk²sΔ/[Γ(1+s+(2Δ/Γ)²)²]
- Doppler limit: T_D = ℏΓ/(2k_B); minimum at Δ = −Γ/2
- Sub-Doppler (Sisyphus) can approach the recoil scale via polarization gradients

**2. Magneto-Optical Trap (MOT)**
- Requires red-detuned laser + quadrupole B-field gradient
- Position-dependent Zeeman shift creates restoring force
- Spring constant κ = α·μ'·(dB/dz)/(ℏk)
- Typical: 10⁸–10¹⁰ atoms, T ~ 10–100 μK

**3. Evaporative Cooling**
- RF knife removes high-energy tail, remaining atoms rethermalize
- Efficiency parameter η = U_trap/(k_BT); need η ≥ 5–10
- Runaway evaporation if PSD gain > atom loss rate
- Reaches BEC at PSD ≥ 2.612 (nλ_dB³ threshold)

**4. Optical Tweezers**
- Focused Gaussian beam: U(r,z) = −U₀/(1+(z/z_R)²) × exp(−2r²/w²(z))
- Trap depth U₀ = (α/2ε₀c) × (P/πw₀²)
- Radial frequency: ω_r = √(4U₀/mw₀²)
- Axial frequency: ω_z = √(2U₀/mz_R²)

**5. Fluorescence Imaging**
- Scatter rate R_sc = (Γ/2)·s/[1+s+(2Δ/Γ)²]
- Photons collected: N = R_sc × t_exp × η_geo × QE × T_opt
- Camera noise: EMCCD (EM gain G, excess noise F=√2), sCMOS (direct read noise)
- SNR = N_sig/√(N_sig + N_bg + σ_read²/G²)

**6. Absorption Imaging**
- Beer-Lambert: I(x,y) = I₀ exp(−n·σ₀·OD)
- Resonant cross-section: σ₀ = 3λ²/(2π) (two-level)
- Destructive — atom is lost after imaging
- Best for large atom number (BEC, thermal cloud)

**7. RF / Microwave Spectroscopy**
- Drive hyperfine transitions at νₕf (Rb: 6.835 GHz, Cs: 9.193 GHz)
- Ramsey spectroscopy: π/2 → free precession → π/2; linewidth ~ 1/T_Ramsey
- Rabi spectroscopy: π pulse for state transfer

**8. Optical Lattices**
- Standing wave creates periodic potential U(x) = U₀ cos²(kx)
- Band structure: Bloch bands, quasi-momentum
- Lattice depth in units of recoil energy E_r = ℏ²k²/(2m)
- Hubbard model: H = −J Σ (c†ᵢcⱼ) + U Σ nᵢ↑nᵢ↓

---

## TOOL 3: RYDBERG CALCULATOR
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/rydberg-calculator.html
**Accent color:** #818cf8 (indigo)

### Physics
Rydberg atoms are highly excited atoms with large principal quantum number n. They exhibit dramatically enhanced properties:

| Property | Scaling | Typical (n=70, Rb) |
|---|---|---|
| Orbital radius | n² | ~260 nm |
| Lifetime τ | n³ | ~300 μs |
| C₆ coefficient | n¹¹ | ~10⁶ GHz·μm⁶ |
| Blockade radius R_b | n^(11/6) | ~10 μm |
| Transition dipole | n² | large |

### Key Formulas

**Lifetime (with BBR correction):**
τ(n) = τ₀ × (n*/n*₀)³ / (1 + Γ_BBR·τ₀·(n*/n*₀)³)

where n* = n − δ_l (quantum defect), and Γ_BBR is the blackbody radiation-induced transition rate at room temperature (300 K).

**van der Waals C₆:**
C₆ ∝ α²(n*)¹¹  [units: GHz·μm⁶]
Interaction: V(R) = C₆/R⁶

**Blockade radius:**
R_b = (C₆/ℏΩ_R)^(1/6)
where Ω_R is the Rabi frequency of the Rydberg excitation laser.

**Gate fidelity estimate:**
ε_se ≈ Γ_Ryd × t_gate     (spontaneous emission during gate)
t_gate ≈ π/Ω_R             (π-pulse time)

### Species Data
| Species | λ_Ryd (nm) | τ₀_ns (reference state) | Quantum defect δ_s |
|---|---|---|---|
| ⁸⁷Rb | 480 | 1.43 | 3.131 |
| ¹³³Cs | 510 | 1.50 | 4.049 |
| ¹⁷¹Yb | 302 | 2.00 | 4.279 |
| ⁸⁸Sr | 317 | 1.80 | 3.269 |

### Chart
Chart.js plot of τ(n) and R_b(n) vs n from 30 to 120 for the selected species.

---

## TOOL 4: IMAGING SNR CALCULATOR
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/imaging-calculator.html
**Accent color:** #34d399 (emerald)

### Physics Model

**Scattering rate:**
R_sc = (Γ/2) × s / (1 + s + (2Δ/Γ)²)    [photons/s]
where s = I/I_sat (saturation parameter), Δ = laser detuning

**Geometric collection efficiency:**
η_geo = (1 − √(1−NA²)) / 2               [exact formula, assumes n=1]
≈ NA²/4                                   [paraxial approximation]

**Total collection efficiency:**
η = η_geo × QE × T_opt
where QE = detector quantum efficiency, T_opt = optical transmission

**Photons detected:**
N_sig = R_sc × t_exp × η

**Background photons:**
N_bg = background_rate × t_exp × η_geo

**Dark counts:**
N_dark = dark_rate × t_exp

**Noise variance:**
- EMCCD:  σ² = F²(N_sig + N_bg + N_dark) + (σ_read/G)²    [F = √2, excess noise]
- sCMOS:  σ² = N_sig + N_bg + N_dark + σ_read²
- Ideal:  σ² = N_sig + N_bg + N_dark

**SNR:**
SNR = N_sig / √σ²

**Detection fidelity:**
F_det ≈ Φ(SNR/2)   [equal-width Gaussian threshold model, SNR = peak separation / σ]
Φ(x) ≈ 1 − φ(x)(b₁t + b₂t² + b₃t³)   [Abramowitz & Stegun approximation]
where t = 1/(1+0.47047x), φ(x) = e^(−x²/2)/√(2π)

### Species Data (D2 line, Steck 2019)
| Species | λ_D2 (nm) | Γ/2π (MHz) | I_sat (mW/cm²) |
|---|---|---|---|
| ⁸⁷Rb | 780.241 | 6.065 | 1.67 |
| ¹³³Cs | 852.347 | 5.234 | 1.10 |
| ⁶Li | 670.977 | 5.872 | 2.54 |
| ⁷Li | 670.977 | 5.872 | 2.54 |
| ²³Na | 589.000 | 9.795 | 6.26 |
| ³⁹K | 766.701 | 6.035 | 1.75 |
| ⁴⁰K | 766.701 | 6.035 | 1.75 |

### Outputs
- R_sc (photons/s), s (saturation parameter), R_max = Γ/2
- η_geo, η_total, N_sig, N_bg, N_dark
- SNR (with bar meter log scale 1–100)
- Detection fidelity F_det
- Noise budget table (signal shot, background shot, dark shot, read noise)
- Min exposure time for SNR=10, SNR=50 (binary search)
- Chart.js: SNR vs exposure time (0.1–100 ms) with reference lines

### Recoil Heating
Single recoil energy: E_rec = (ℏk)²/(2m)
Recoil heating rate: dT/dt ≈ 2E_rec R_sc/k_B   [factor 2: absorption + emission, no cooling]

---

## TOOL 5: TOF THERMOMETRY
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/tof-calculator.html
**Accent color:** #38bdf8 (sky blue)

### Physics

**Ballistic expansion (free flight after trap release):**
σ²(t) = σ₀² + (k_BT/m)·t²
- σ₀ = in-situ cloud radius (initial size)
- Linear in t² → temperature from slope

**de Broglie wavelength:**
λ_dB = h / √(2πmk_BT)

**Peak number density:**
n_peak = N / (2π)^(3/2) / σ_x σ_y σ_z      [Gaussian cloud]

**Phase-space density:**
PSD = n_peak × λ_dB³
BEC threshold: PSD ≥ 2.612   (for ideal Bose gas in 3D harmonic trap)

**Cooling limits:**
T_Doppler = ℏΓ/(2k_B)     [minimum achievable with Doppler cooling]
E_recoil/k_B = ℏ²k²/(2mk_B)   [single-photon recoil energy as temperature]

**In-trap thermal size (if trap frequency known):**
σ_thermal = √(k_BT/(mω²))

### Fit From Data (linear regression)
Given N data points (tᵢ, σᵢ):
- Linearize: Yᵢ = σᵢ², Xᵢ = tᵢ²
- Slope m = Σ(Xᵢ-X̄)(Yᵢ-Ȳ) / Σ(Xᵢ-X̄)²
- Intercept b = Ȳ − m·X̄
- T = m·(mass)/(k_B)   [from slope = k_BT/m]
- σ₀ = √b             [from intercept = σ₀²]
- R² = 1 − SS_res/SS_tot

### Species Data (exact masses)
| Species | Mass (u) |
|---|---|
| ⁸⁵Rb | 84.912 |
| ⁸⁷Rb | 86.909 |
| ¹³³Cs | 132.905 |
| ⁶Li | 6.015 |
| ⁷Li | 7.016 |
| ²³Na | 22.990 |
| ³⁹K | 38.964 |
| ⁴⁰K | 39.964 |
| ⁴¹K | 40.962 |

### Charts
1. σ(t) vs t — expansion curve with free-expansion asymptote and σ₀ reference
2. σ² vs t² — scatter plot with linear fit line (from data table)

---

## TOOL 6: MOT / MAGNETIC TRAP DESIGNER
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/mot-designer.html
**Accent color:** #fb923c (orange)

### Section 1: MOT Parameters

**Radiation pressure force on a two-level atom:**
F = ℏk·(Γ/2)·s / (1 + s + (2(Δ−kv)/Γ)²) − ℏk·(Γ/2)·s / (1 + s + (2(Δ+kv)/Γ)²)

**Damping coefficient (first-order in v):**
α = 8ℏk²·s·|Δ|/Γ / (1 + s + (2Δ/Γ)²)²   [units: kg/s]

**MOT spring constant (with B-field gradient):**
κ = α·μ_eff·(dB/dz) / (ℏk)               [units: N/m]

**MOT oscillation frequency:**
ω_MOT = √(κ/m)

**Capture velocity:**
v_cap ≈ w_beam × |α|/(2m)                 [beam radius w_beam]

**Doppler temperature (theoretical minimum):**
T_D = ℏΓ/(2k_B)                           [Rb: 145 μK, Cs: 125 μK]

**Maximum deceleration:**
a_max = ℏk·Γ/(2m)                         [at saturation s→∞]

### Section 2: Ioffe-Pritchard Magnetic Trap

The IP trap has a non-zero field minimum B₀ at the trap center (prevents Majorana flips).

**Radial (transverse) frequency:**
ω_r = √(μ_eff·B'² / (m·B₀))              [B' = radial gradient]

**Axial frequency:**
ω_z = √(μ_eff·B'' / m)                    [B'' = axial curvature]

**Mean trap frequency:**
ω̄ = (ω_r²·ω_z)^(1/3)

**Trap depth:**
U₀ = μ_eff·B_max                          [B_max = field at trap edge]

**Evaporation parameter:**
η = U₀/(k_BT)
Good evaporation: η ≥ 5; runaway evaporation: η ≥ 8–10

**Majorana loss warning:**
Majorana spin-flip rate: Γ_M ∝ ω_r² × exp(−2μB₀/ℏω_r)
Warning triggered when B₀ < 3 G (high loss risk)

**Effective magnetic moment:**
μ_eff = m_F × g_F × μ_B
For ⁸⁷Rb |F=2, m_F=2⟩: μ_eff = 2 × (1/2) × μ_B = μ_B

### Chart
U(r) = μ_eff × |B(r)| where |B(r)| = √((B'r)² + B₀²)
Displayed with k_BT and U₀ horizontal reference lines.

---

## TOOL 7: GATE FIDELITY BUDGET
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/fidelity-budget.html
**Accent color:** #f87171 (red)

### Overview
Calculates the two-qubit gate fidelity for Rydberg neutral atom quantum computers by budgeting 8 independent error sources.

### Error Sources and Formulas

**1. Spontaneous emission from Rydberg state:**
ε_se = Γ_Ryd × t_gate
where Γ_Ryd = 1/τ_Ryd (BBR-corrected lifetime), t_gate = π/Ω_R

**2. Doppler dephasing (thermal motion):**
ε_D = ½ × (k_Ryd × v_rms × t_gate)²
v_rms = √(k_BT/m), k_Ryd = 2π/λ_Ryd

**3. Laser phase noise (finite linewidth):**
ε_φ = π × Δν_laser × t_gate
Δν_laser = laser linewidth (Hz)

**4. Finite Rydberg blockade (leakage):**
ε_blk = 1 / (U_dd/Ω_R)²
U_dd = blockade interaction strength; need U_dd ≫ Ω_R

**5. State preparation and measurement (SPAM):**
ε_SPAM = SPAM_error (%)     [typically 0.5–2%]

**6. Atom loss during gate:**
ε_loss = t_gate / τ_trap    [trap lifetime τ_trap ~ 1–10 s]

**7. Magnetic field noise (dephasing):**
ε_B = ½ × (μ_B × ΔB × t_gate / ℏ)²
ΔB = rms field fluctuation (mG)

**8. Rabi frequency inhomogeneity:**
ε_Ω = ½ × (π × δΩ/Ω / 2)²
δΩ/Ω = fractional Rabi frequency variation across atomic ensemble

**Total error:**
ε_total = Σᵢ εᵢ    [to first order, errors add]

**Gate fidelity:**
F_gate = 1 − ε_total

### Fidelity Meter
- F ≥ 99.9%: green (fault-tolerant threshold for many QEC codes)
- F ≥ 99.0%: yellow (surface code threshold ≈ 99%)
- F < 99.0%: red (below practical threshold)

### SOTA Comparison
| Reference | F_gate | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Evered et al. 2023 (Nature) | 99.5% | Rb tweezer, CZ gate |
| Muniz et al. 2025 (PRX Quantum) | 99.72% post-selected / 99.40% raw | ¹⁷¹Yb nuclear-spin CZ |
| Ma et al. 2023 (Nature) | 98.0% | metastable ¹⁷¹Yb with erasure conversion |
| Levine et al. 2022 (PRX Quantum) | 99.0% | Rb, parallel gates |
| FTQC threshold | 99.0% | Surface code estimate |
| Typical lab (2024) | 97–99% | Varies by implementation |

### BBR Correction to Rydberg Lifetime
At room temperature T=300K, blackbody radiation drives transitions to neighboring Rydberg states:
τ_eff = τ₀ × n*³ / (1 + α_BBR × (T/300K)⁴ × n*²)

---

## TOOL 8: RELEASE-RECAPTURE SIMULATOR
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/release-recapture.html
**Accent color:** #fb923c (orange)

Simulates the release-recapture technique for estimating atom temperature in optical tweezers or dipole traps with known waist and depth:
1. Atom released from tweezer (trap turned off)
2. Atom evolves under gravity + initial thermal velocity
3. Trap turned back on after time τ
4. Atom recaptured if within trap volume (r < w₀, z < z_R)

Recapture probability P(τ) vs hold time τ decreases as hotter atoms expand beyond the recapture region.
This is thermometry for a known potential, not a direct trap-frequency measurement; use parametric heating or sideband spectroscopy for direct frequency calibration.

---

## TOOL 9: LAB CALCULATORS
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/lab-calculators.html
**Accent color:** #fbbf24 (amber)

### Optics Calculators
- **Beam waist:** w(z) = w₀√(1+(z/z_R)²), z_R = πw₀²/λ
- **Numerical aperture:** NA = n·sin(θ), w₀ = λ/(π·NA) for diffraction-limited focus
- **Fiber coupling:** η_fiber = exp(−2(Δr/w₀)²) × cos²(Δθ·kw₀/2)
- **Telescope magnification:** M = f₂/f₁, w_out = M·w_in
- **f-number:** f/# = f/D = 1/(2·NA)
- **Power (dBm ↔ mW):** P_mW = 10^(P_dBm/10), P_dBm = 10·log₁₀(P_mW)
- **AOM shift:** ν_out = ν_in ± ν_AOM; double-pass: ±2ν_AOM
- **Shot noise:** σ_shot = √N (photon number), RIN = 2ℏω/P

### Atomic Physics Calculators
- **Photon recoil velocity:** v_r = ℏk/m
- **Single recoil scale:** E_rec/k_B = ℏ²k²/(2mk_B); one scatter cycle is roughly 2E_rec/k_B without cooling
- **Doppler temperature:** T_D = ℏΓ/(2k_B)
- **de Broglie wavelength:** λ_dB = h/√(2πmk_BT)
- **Zeeman shift:** ΔE = m_F·g_F·μ_B·B (linear Zeeman)
- **Saturation intensity:** I_sat = πhcΓ/(3λ³)
- **Mean thermal velocity:** v̄ = √(8k_BT/πm)
- **RMS velocity:** v_rms = √(3k_BT/m)

### Trap Calculators
- **Tweezer radial frequency:** ω_r = √(4U₀/(mw₀²))
- **Tweezer axial frequency:** ω_z = √(2U₀/(mz_R²)) = ω_r·w₀/z_R·√(1/2)
- **Lamb-Dicke parameter:** η_LD = k·x_zpf = k·√(ℏ/(2mω))
- **Zero-point fluctuation:** x_zpf = √(ℏ/(2mω))
- **Cavity FSR:** ν_FSR = c/(2nL)
- **Cavity finesse:** F = π√R/(1-R) (R = mirror reflectivity)
- **Cavity linewidth:** δν_cav = ν_FSR/F
- **Mode matching:** η_mm = |⟨Gaussian_1|Gaussian_2⟩|²

### Clebsch-Gordan Coefficients
⟨j₁m₁;j₂m₂|JM⟩ computed via Wigner 3j symbol:
⟨j₁m₁;j₂m₂|JM⟩ = (-1)^(j₁-j₂+M)·√(2J+1)·(j₁ j₂ J; m₁ m₂ -M)

---

## TOOL 10: LASER LOCKING GUIDE
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/laser-locking.html
**Accent color:** #f87171 (red)

### Saturated Absorption Spectroscopy (SAS)
- Pump beam saturates the transition; probe sees Doppler-free dip at resonance
- Error signal: derivative of absorption dip (side-of-fringe locking)
- Linewidth ~ Γ/2π × √(1 + I/I_sat) (power-broadened)
- Crossover resonances: ν_cross = (ν_a + ν_b)/2

### Beat-Note Offset Locking
- Two lasers mixed on fast photodetector
- Beat note: i_PD(t) ∝ E₁E₂·cos[2π(ν₂−ν₁)t + Δφ]
- Lock one laser's frequency relative to other: ν₂ = ν₁ ± ν_offset

### PDH (Pound-Drever-Hall) Locking
- Phase modulation at frequency Ω_m creates sidebands at ν ± Ω_m
- Cavity reflection field: E_r = E₀[r(ν)·J₀(β) + ir(ν+Ω_m)J₁(β)e^{iΩt} − ir(ν−Ω_m)J₁(β)e^{−iΩt}]
- Error signal (demodulated at Ω_m):
  ε(ν) = −2√(P_c P_s)·J₀(β)·J₁(β)·Im[F(ν)]
  where P_c = carrier power, P_s = sideband power, F(ν) = cavity reflection coefficient

### Cavity Parameters
- FSR: ν_FSR = c/(2nL)
- Finesse: F = π√R/(1−R) ≈ π/(1−R) for R ≈ 1
- Cavity linewidth: δν_cav = ν_FSR/F
- Reflection coefficient: r(ν) = (r_1 − r_2·e^{2iπν/ν_FSR})/(1 − r_1r_2·e^{2iπν/ν_FSR})

---

## TOOL 11: ZERNIKE POLYNOMIAL VISUALIZER
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/zernike.html
**Accent color:** #2dd4bf (teal)

### OSA/ANSI Zernike Polynomials
Z_n^m(ρ,θ) = R_n^|m|(ρ) × Θ_m(θ)

**Radial polynomial:**
R_n^|m|(ρ) = Σ_{s=0}^{(n-|m|)/2} [(-1)^s (n−s)! / (s! ((n+|m|)/2−s)! ((n−|m|)/2−s)!)] × ρ^{n-2s}

**Angular part:**
Θ_m(θ) = √2·cos(mθ)  for m > 0
          1            for m = 0
          √2·sin(|m|θ) for m < 0

### OSA/ANSI Single-Index Terms (j-ordering)
j=0: piston Z₀⁰   j=1: tip Z₁⁻¹    j=2: tilt Z₁¹
j=3: defocus Z₂⁰  j=4: astigmatism Z₂⁻²  j=5: astigmatism Z₂²
j=6: coma Z₃⁻¹    j=7: coma Z₃¹    j=8: trefoil Z₃⁻³  j=9: trefoil Z₃³
j=10: spherical Z₄⁰  j=11: secondary astigmatism ...

### Strehl Ratio
For small aberrations (Maréchal criterion):
S ≈ exp(−σ²)   where σ² = variance of wavefront across pupil [in waves²]
S ≥ 0.8 → diffraction-limited (Strehl criterion)

### SLM Application (LG modes)
Laguerre-Gaussian LG_p^l modes generated by SLM phase pattern:
φ(r,θ) = l·θ + (2p+|l|+1)·arctan(z/z_R) + correction terms for beam shaping

---

## TOOL 12: POLARIMETRY EXPLORER
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/polarimetry.html
**Accent color:** #e879f9 (fuchsia)

### Stokes Parameters
S₀ = total intensity
S₁ = I(0°) − I(90°)
S₂ = I(45°) − I(135°)
S₃ = I_RCP − I_LCP

Degree of polarization: P = √(S₁² + S₂² + S₃²)/S₀

### Quarter-Wave Plate (rotating) Method
Intensity vs QWP angle α:
I(α) = (S₀/2)[1 + S₁/S₀·cos²(2α) + S₂/S₀·cos(2α)sin(2α) + S₃/S₀·(1/2)sin(4α − π)]

### Retardance Measurement
Phase retardance δ: Δφ between fast and slow axes
QWP: δ = π/2; HWP: δ = π
Measure via rotating-waveplate Stokes polarimetry

---

## TOOL 13: LASER SYSTEM PLANNER
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/laser-planner.html
**Accent color:** #f472b6 (pink)

Design tool, not a calculator: pick an atom species and get the laser system needed to run it.
### Sections
01 Select Atom Species — 02 Required Laser Beams (cooling, repump, imaging, Rydberg/clock as applicable, with wavelengths) — 03 System Block Diagram (source → AOM/EOM → fiber → chamber) — 04 Nonlinear Optics Required (SHG/frequency-doubling stages needed to reach each wavelength from available diode/fiber laser lines)
Output is species-specific: which beams you need, their wavelengths, and what nonlinear-optics hardware bridges the gap from a stock diode/fiber laser to that wavelength.

---

## TOOL 14: CAVITY QED COUPLING LAB
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/cavity-qed.html
**Accent color:** #c084fc (violet)

**Single-photon coupling rate:**
$$g_0 = \xi\sqrt{\frac{3\Gamma c^3}{2\omega^2 V}} \qquad V = \frac{\pi w_0^2 L}{4} \qquad C = \frac{g_0^2}{\kappa\gamma} \qquad \kappa = \frac{\text{FSR}}{2\mathcal{F}} \qquad \gamma = \frac{\Gamma}{2}$$

**Strong-coupling condition:**
$$\text{Strong coupling:} \quad g_0 > \kappa,\;\gamma \quad \Leftrightarrow \quad \mathcal{F} > \frac{\pi c}{4 L g_0}$$

Classifies a given atom-cavity system into strong / weak / bad-cavity coupling regimes given cavity length L, waist w₀, finesse F, and atomic linewidth Γ; charts coupling rate vs finesse; includes reference systems and a common-mistakes section.

---

## TOOL 15: VACUUM SYSTEMS GUIDE
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/vacuum-systems.html
**Accent color:** #22d3ee (cyan)

Reference guide, not a calculator, for why UHV matters in cold-atom experiments.
### Sections
01 Why Vacuum? — 02 The Pressure Landscape (rough/high/ultra-high regimes) — 03 The Pumping Chain (roughing, turbo, ion, getter pumps) — 04 Baking Out — 05 Materials & Connections (conflat flanges, leak checking) — 06 Reading Your Vacuum (gauge types) — 07 Practical Checklist

**Mean free path (kinetic theory):**
$$\lambda = \frac{k_B T}{\sqrt{2}\,\pi\,d^2\,P}$$

---

## TOOL 16: TWEEZER ARRAY DESIGN LAB
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/tweezer-designer.html
**Accent color:** #f59e0b (amber)

**Gaussian-beam trap depth (far-detuned dipole trap):**
$$U_0 = -\frac{3\pi c^2}{2\omega_0^3}\,\frac{\Gamma}{\Delta}\,I_0$$

**Radial trap frequency:**
$$\omega_r = \sqrt{\frac{4|U_0|}{m w_0^2}}$$

### Sections
01 Single-Tweezer Physics (waist, depth, trap frequency) — 02 Trap Parameter Calculator — 03 Array Geometry (spacing, SLM/AOD generation) — 04 Species & Wavelength Reference (common tweezer-trapped atoms) — 05 References & Further Reading

---

## TOOL 17: ABSORPTION IMAGING LAB
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/absorption-imaging.html
**Accent color:** #818cf8 (indigo)

Companion to the fluorescence Imaging SNR Calculator, focused on Beer-Lambert absorption imaging.

**Measured optical density:**
$$\text{OD} = -\ln\!\left(\frac{I_{\rm shadow} - I_{\rm dark}}{I_{\rm probe} - I_{\rm dark}}\right)$$

**Saturation-corrected optical density (accounts for high-intensity/near-resonant imaging):**
$$\text{OD}_{\rm true} = \text{OD}_{\rm meas} + \frac{I_{\rm probe} - I_{\rm shadow}}{I_{\rm sat}}$$
$$\text{OD}_{\rm true} = -\ln\!\left(\frac{I_{\rm out}}{I_{\rm in}}\right) + \frac{I_{\rm in} - I_{\rm out}}{I_{\rm sat}}$$

### Sections
01 How Absorption Imaging Works — 02 Imaging Calculator — 03 Species Reference — 04 Practical Considerations (resonant vs off-resonant, saturation effects, high-intensity imaging) — 05 References & Further Reading

---

## TOOL 18: RANDOMIZED BENCHMARKING EXPLORER
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/rb-explorer.html
**Accent color:** #c084fc (violet)

Not about rubidium — RB as in the gate-fidelity-characterization protocol. Covers the SPAM problem and Clifford-group twirling.

**Exponential decay model:**
$$F(m) = A \cdot p^m + B$$

**Average error rate from decay parameter p (dimension d, with 1-/2-qubit special cases):**
$$r_C = \frac{(d-1)(1-p)}{d} \quad \text{[1Q: } r_C = \tfrac{1-p}{2}\text{, 2Q: } r_C = \tfrac{3(1-p)}{4}\text{]}$$

**Per-gate error estimate:**
$$\varepsilon_{\rm gate} \approx \frac{r_C}{\bar{n}} \quad \text{where } \bar{n} \approx 1.875 \text{ (1Q)}, \approx 2.0 \text{ (2Q)}$$

**Useful circuit depth (fidelity ≈ 1/e):**
$$N_{\rm useful} = \frac{1}{2r_C}$$

**Interleaved RB (IRB) — isolating a single gate's error:**
$$r_{\rm gate} = \frac{d-1}{d}\left(1 - \frac{p_{\rm int}}{p_{\rm ref}}\right)$$

Includes working calculators for both standard RB and interleaved RB, plus SOTA neutral-atom results.

---

## TOOL 19: DYNAMICAL DECOUPLING PLAYGROUND
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/dd-playground.html
**Accent color:** #4ade80 (green)

Interactive playground for qubit coherence and π-pulse decoupling sequences.

**Free-induction decay — Gaussian dephasing vs Markovian/exponential:**
$$W_{\rm FID}(T) = e^{-(T/T_2^*)^2} \qquad W_{\rm FID}(T) = e^{-T/T_2^*}$$

**General dephasing via noise filter function:**
$$\chi(T) = \frac{1}{2\pi}\int_0^\infty S(\omega)\,|\tilde{Y}(\omega,T)|^2\,d\omega, \qquad T_2 \text{ defined by } \chi(T_2) = 1$$

**Hahn echo filter function:**
$$|\tilde{Y}_{\rm HE}(\omega,T)|^2 \propto \frac{\sin^4(\omega T/4)}{\omega^2}$$

**CPMG pulse timing and filter function (N pulses):**
$$t_k^{\rm CPMG} = \frac{(2k-1)\,T}{2N}, \quad k = 1,\ldots,N$$
$$|\tilde{Y}_{\rm CPMG-N}(\omega,T)|^2 = \frac{4\sin^2(\omega\tau)\sin^2(N\omega\tau)}{\omega^2}, \quad \tau = \frac{T}{2N}$$

**CPMG scaling under power-law noise S(ω) ∝ ω⁻ᵅ:**
$$T_2^{\rm CPMG}(N) \propto N^{\alpha/(1+\alpha)}$$

**Noise-spectroscopy inversion (extracting S(ω) from a DD scan):**
$$S(\omega_c) \approx \frac{\chi(T,N)}{|\tilde{Y}(\omega_c,T)|^2 \Delta\omega_{\rm peak}}$$

Shows how DD sequences (Hahn echo, CPMG, etc.) act as spectral filter functions that extend coherence by refocusing low-frequency noise.

---

## TOOL 20: REMOTE ENTANGLEMENT
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/remote-entanglement.html
**Accent color:** #0ea5e9 (sky)

Deep dive on photon-mediated entanglement between physically separated qubits/tweezer modules — connecting independent quantum processors, not single-qubit fidelity, is framed as the scaling bottleneck.

**Heralded entanglement success probability (single-photon collection, both sides):**
$$p_{\rm success} = \frac{\eta_A \cdot \eta_B}{2}$$

**Photon collection efficiency (NA-limited):**
$$\eta_{\rm coll} = \frac{1 - \sqrt{1-\text{NA}^2}}{2} \approx \frac{\text{NA}^2}{4}$$

**Bell-state projection after a Bell-state measurement (BSM):**
$$|\Psi_{\rm after\ BSM}\rangle = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\!\left(|{\uparrow}_A\, {\uparrow}_B\rangle + e^{i\theta}|{\downarrow}_A\, {\downarrow}_B\rangle\right) = |\Phi^+\rangle \;\;\text{or}\;\; |\Phi^-\rangle$$

**Fidelity bound from HOM-type interference visibility M:**
$$\mathcal{F} \leq \frac{1 + M}{2}$$

**Entanglement generation rate:**
$$\text{duty cycle} = p_{\rm success} \times f_{\rm rep} \times \tau_{\rm attempt}$$

Covers heralded entanglement protocols, photon collection/loss budgets, and reference experimental results linking remote atoms via optical fiber/free-space photon links.

---

## TOOL 21: COOLING SIMULATOR (expanded)
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/cooling-simulator.html
**Accent color:** #60a5fa (blue)

**Scattering rate:**
$$\Gamma_{\rm sc} = \frac{\Gamma}{2}\cdot\frac{s_0}{1 + s_0 + (2\delta'/\Gamma)^2}$$

**Doppler cooling force and damping coefficient:**
$$F(v) \approx -\alpha v, \qquad \alpha = \hbar k^2 \cdot \frac{4|\delta|\,s_0\,\Gamma}{(1 + s_0 + (2\delta/\Gamma)^2)^2}$$

**Doppler temperature limit:**
$$T_D = \frac{\hbar\Gamma}{2k_{\rm B}}$$

**Sisyphus (sub-Doppler) temperature scaling:**
$$T_{\rm Sisyphus} \propto \frac{U_0^2}{E_r} \propto \frac{I^2}{\delta^2\,E_r}$$

**Gray-molasses dark state:**
$$|\mathrm{dark}\rangle = \cos\theta\,|F\!=\!1/2\rangle - \sin\theta\,|F\!=\!3/2\rangle$$

Interactive Doppler + Sisyphus cooling animation; velocity distribution narrows over time; equilibrium temperature k_BT = D_p/|α| where D_p is the momentum-diffusion coefficient.

---

## TOOL 22: SINGLE-ATOM COOLING (deep dive)
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/laser-cooling.html
**Accent color:** #60a5fa (blue)

Narrative/derivation companion to the Cooling Simulator — same physics, textbook-style walkthrough rather than an interactive tool. Includes the resolved-sideband and gray-molasses cooling animations (GIFs) as visual aids.

**Laser linewidth requirement for resolved-sideband cooling:**
$$\delta\nu_\text{laser} \lesssim \eta\, \Omega_R \lesssim \eta\, \nu$$

---

## CAREER & REFERENCE PAGES

### AMO Group Finder (`pages/amo-groups.html`)
**Accent color:** #4ade80 (green)
Career-mapping tool: a directory of 100+ AMO research groups. Sections: 02 Featured Directory (most relevant groups, deeply profiled), 03 Global Watchlist (broader list), 04 Highest-Priority Shortlists (narrowing down where to apply for grad school/postdoc positions by subfield — ultracold atoms, Rydberg, cavity QED, precision measurement, etc.).

### AMO Paper Roadmap (`pages/paper-syllabus.html`)
**Accent color:** #818cf8 (indigo)
"53 Papers That Build an AMO Career" — a curated reading list organized by career stage, presented as 4 tabs: 🎓 Undergrad Entry, 🔬 Grad Year 1, ⚛️ Advanced Grad, 🚀 Postdoc/Career. Guides a physicist through foundational and current AMO/QC literature in order, with explicit guidance not to read all 53 papers the same way.

### Rb87 vs Yb171 Qubit Comparison (`pages/rb87-vs-yb171.html`)
**Accent color:** #f59e0b (amber)
Side-by-side platform comparison (Rb87 alkali vs Yb171 alkaline-earth-like) for choosing a qubit species. 12 sections: Why These Two Atoms, Atomic Physics Deep Dive, Qubit Encoding, Laser Cooling & State Prep, Coherence & Decoherence, Gate Performance, Readout & SPAM, Array Size & Scalability, Erasure Conversion (Yb171's structural advantage), Full Head-to-Head Comparison, Companies & Ecosystem, Key Papers & References.

Sample qubit encodings:
$$|0\rangle = |F{=}1, m_F{=}0\rangle, \quad |1\rangle = |F{=}2, m_F{=}0\rangle \quad \text{(Rb87 clock states)}$$
$$|0\rangle = |m_I{=}-\tfrac{1}{2}\rangle, \quad |1\rangle = |m_I{=}+\tfrac{1}{2}\rangle \quad \text{(Yb171 nuclear-spin qubit)}$$

Gate fidelity decomposition used for the head-to-head comparison:
$$\mathcal{F}_{\rm CZ} = 1 - \varepsilon_{\rm Ryd} - \varepsilon_{\rm scatt} - \varepsilon_{\rm D} - \varepsilon_{\rm \Omega}$$

Published benchmarks cited: Rb87 CZ fidelity 99.5% (up to 60 parallel atom pairs); Yb171 nuclear-spin qubit differential Zeeman shift ≈ 2.6 Hz/mG.

---

## QC INDUSTRY LANDSCAPE
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/qc-landscape.html
**Accent color:** #a78bfa (violet)

### Hardware Platforms Comparison
| Platform | Companies | AMO relevance |
|---|---|---|
| Trapped ions | IonQ, Quantinuum (H2), AQT, Alpine Quantum Technologies | Direct — laser cooling, laser control, vacuum |
| Neutral atoms | QuEra (Rb tweezer), Pasqal, Atom Computing, Infleqtion | Direct — MOT, tweezer, Rydberg |
| Superconducting | IBM, Google (Sycamore), Rigetti, IQM | Indirect — microwave, cryogenics |
| Photonic | PsiQuantum, Xanadu | Indirect — optics, nonlinear |
| Spin qubits | Intel, QuTech | Indirect — some AMO laser skills |

### Job Roles for AMO Physicists
- **Quantum hardware engineer:** Trap design, vacuum, laser systems, control electronics
- **Algorithm/software engineer:** Circuit optimization, error correction, simulation
- **Applications scientist:** Customer use cases, quantum advantage demonstrations
- **Research scientist:** Novel qubit modalities, gate fidelity improvement
- **Systems engineer:** Integration, scaling, reliability

### AMO Skills That Transfer
Optical tweezers → single qubit addressing; MOT loading → qubit initialization; PDH locking → laser coherence for gates; fluorescence imaging → qubit readout; Rydberg physics → gate mechanism (QuEra, Pasqal, Atom Computing, Infleqtion)

---

## LEARN QUANTUM — ALL 14 TOPICS
**URL:** https://amotoolkit.com/pages/learn-quantum.html

### 1. Bloch Sphere (#bloch)
Single qubit state: |ψ⟩ = cos(θ/2)|0⟩ + e^{iφ}sin(θ/2)|1⟩
Measurement probabilities: P(|0⟩) = cos²(θ/2), P(|1⟩) = sin²(θ/2)
Bloch vector: ⟨σ_x⟩ = sinθ·cosφ, ⟨σ_y⟩ = sinθ·sinφ, ⟨σ_z⟩ = cosθ
Mach-Zehnder unitary: U_MZI = H·R_z(φ)·H
Output: P(|0⟩) = cos²(φ/2), P(|1⟩) = sin²(φ/2)

### 2. Quantum Gates (#gates)
Single-qubit gates (2×2 unitary matrices):
- Hadamard: H = (1/√2)[[1,1],[1,-1]]
- Pauli-X: [[0,1],[1,0]] (bit flip)
- Pauli-Y: [[0,-i],[i,0]]
- Pauli-Z: [[1,0],[0,-1]] (phase flip)
- S gate: [[1,0],[0,i]] (phase = π/2)
- T gate: [[1,0],[0,e^{iπ/4}]] (phase = π/4, needed for universality)
- R_z(θ): [[e^{-iθ/2},0],[0,e^{iθ/2}]]

Two-qubit gates:
- CNOT: flips target if control = |1⟩
- CZ: phase flip if both qubits |1⟩; CZ = (H⊗I)·CNOT·(H⊗I)
- SWAP: exchanges two qubit states; SWAP = 3 CNOTs

Universality: {H, T, CNOT} is a universal gate set

### 3. Superposition (#superposition)
|ψ⟩ = α|0⟩ + β|1⟩, |α|² + |β|² = 1
Born rule: P(outcome) = |amplitude|²
Interference: amplitudes add (constructively or destructively)
Measurement collapses superposition — process is irreversible

### 4. Quantum Measurement (#measurement)
Projective measurement on basis {|0⟩,|1⟩}:
P(0) = |⟨0|ψ⟩|² = |α|², post-measurement state: |0⟩
Expectation value: ⟨O⟩ = ⟨ψ|O|ψ⟩
No-cloning theorem: cannot copy unknown quantum state |ψ⟩
Quantum Zeno effect: frequent measurement suppresses evolution

### 5. Entanglement (#entanglement)
Bell states (maximally entangled two-qubit states):
|Φ⁺⟩ = (|00⟩ + |11⟩)/√2    |Φ⁻⟩ = (|00⟩ − |11⟩)/√2
|Ψ⁺⟩ = (|01⟩ + |10⟩)/√2    |Ψ⁻⟩ = (|01⟩ − |10⟩)/√2

EPR paradox: Einstein-Podolsky-Rosen — apparent non-locality
CHSH inequality: |⟨AB⟩ + ⟨AB'⟩ + ⟨A'B⟩ − ⟨A'B'⟩| ≤ 2 (classical)
Quantum violation: up to 2√2 ≈ 2.83

Quantum teleportation protocol:
1. Share Bell pair between Alice (A) and Bob (B)
2. Alice has unknown state |ψ⟩; applies CNOT + H
3. Alice measures 2 bits, sends to Bob classically
4. Bob applies X^m₂·Z^m₁ to recover |ψ⟩
No information travels faster than light (classical channel needed)

### 6. Rydberg Atoms (#rydberg)
n-scaling summary:
- Orbital radius: a₀·n² (Bohr model) → nm scale for n ~ 50
- Lifetime: τ₀·n³ → μs scale
- van der Waals C₆: ∝ n¹¹
- Blockade radius R_b ∝ n^(11/6): typically 5–15 μm

Dipole-dipole interaction: V(R) = C₆/R⁶ (van der Waals regime, large R)
Blockade: if two atoms within R_b, second excitation is blockaded (energy mismatch = U_dd ≫ ℏΩ)
Blockade enables entangling gate: one excitation controls the other

### 7. Two-Qubit Gates (#twoqubit)
**Rydberg CZ gate (blockade protocol):**
1. π pulse on control (|0⟩ → |r⟩, |1⟩ stays)
2. 2π pulse on target (if control in |r⟩, target blocked; if control in |0⟩, target rotates 2π → phase)
3. π pulse on control (|r⟩ → |0⟩)
Result: |11⟩ → e^{iπ}|11⟩ = −|11⟩ (CZ phase)

**Mølmer-Sørensen gate (trapped ions):**
H_MS = ℏ·η·Ω·(σ₊ₐ + σ₋ₐ)(a + a†) + h.c.
Entangles two ions through shared motional mode
Generates: exp(−iπ/4·σ_xᵃ⊗σ_xᵇ)

### 8. Rabi Oscillations (#rabi)
Two-level atom coupled to near-resonant field (Δ = ω_laser − ω_atom):
H = ℏ/2 × [[−Δ, Ω*],[Ω, Δ]]

Generalized Rabi frequency: Ω' = √(Ω² + Δ²)
Excited state population: P_e(t) = (Ω/Ω')²·sin²(Ω't/2)
On resonance (Δ=0): P_e(t) = sin²(Ωt/2) — full oscillation

Dressed states (energy eigenstates in rotating frame):
|+⟩ = cos(θ/2)|e⟩ + sin(θ/2)|g⟩
|−⟩ = −sin(θ/2)|e⟩ + cos(θ/2)|g⟩
where tan(θ) = Ω/Δ

π pulse: t = π/Ω → full population transfer |g⟩ → |e⟩
π/2 pulse: creates equal superposition

### 9. Decoherence (#decoherence)
T₁ (longitudinal relaxation / energy decay):
ρ_ee(t) = ρ_ee(0)·e^{−t/T₁}
Qubit loses energy to environment (spontaneous emission, phonons)

T₂ (transverse relaxation / dephasing):
ρ_ge(t) = ρ_ge(0)·e^{−t/T₂}
T₂ ≤ 2T₁ (fundamental bound)

T₂* (inhomogeneous dephasing):
Faster apparent dephasing from static field inhomogeneity
Reversible with spin echo: π/2 − τ − π − τ − π/2

Refocusing sequences:
Hahn echo: π/2 − τ − π − τ − π/2, recovers T₂ from T₂*
CPMG: repeated π pulses, extends T₂ against slow noise

### 10. Hyperfine Qubits (#hyperfine)
Hyperfine Hamiltonian:
H_hf = A_hf·(I·J) + B_hf·[3(I·J)² − I(I+1)J(J+1)]/[2I(2I−1)J(2J−1)]

Total angular momentum: F = I + J, with |I−J| ≤ F ≤ I+J
Energy levels: E_F = (A_hf/2)·[F(F+1) − I(I+1) − J(J+1)]

Clock states (first-order field-insensitive m_F=0 ↔ m_F=0):
⁸⁷Rb: |F=1,m_F=0⟩ ↔ |F=2,m_F=0⟩ at 6.834683 GHz
¹³³Cs: |F=3,m_F=0⟩ ↔ |F=4,m_F=0⟩ at 9.192631770 GHz (SI second definition)

Qubit transitions for ⁸⁷Rb (magnetically sensitive):
|F=1,m_F=−1⟩ ↔ |F=2,m_F=+1⟩ used in some experiments (allows MW + RF addressing)

Zeeman shift (linear): ΔE = m_F·g_F·μ_B·B
⁸⁷Rb F=2: g_F = +1/2, sensitivity ~ 700 kHz/G
⁸⁷Rb F=1: g_F = −1/2

### 11. Optical Pumping (#optical-pumping)
σ⁺ light selection rule: Δm_F = +1 (drives atom toward higher m_F)
Dark state: m_F = F_max (cannot absorb σ⁺ and decay to lower m_F)

For F=1 ground state with σ⁺ pumping (3 sublevels: m_F = −1, 0, +1):
Transition rates (phenomenological 3×3 matrix):
- From m_F=−1: 50% to m_F=0, 50% to m_F=+1 (via absorption + spontaneous)
- From m_F=0: 25% to m_F=0, 75% to m_F=+1
- From m_F=+1: 0% change (dark state)

After ~20 photon scattering events: population > 95% in m_F=+1 stretch state.

EIT / CPT dark states:
For Λ-system (|1⟩, |2⟩ ground states + |e⟩ excited):
Dark state: |D⟩ = (Ω₂|1⟩ − Ω₁|2⟩)/√(Ω₁² + Ω₂²)
Coherent population trapping when two-photon resonance: ν₁ − ν₂ = ν_hf

Applications: clock-state preparation (reset after measurement), spin squeezing, atomic magnetometry.

### 12. Quantum Error Correction (#qec)
3-qubit bit-flip code:
|0⟩_L = |000⟩, |1⟩_L = |111⟩
Detects (not corrects) phase errors; corrects single bit-flips
Syndrome measurement: ZZ⊗I, I⊗ZZ → identifies which qubit flipped

Shor 9-qubit code:
Encodes 1 logical qubit in 9 physical qubits
Corrects any single-qubit error (bit flip, phase, or combination)
First quantum error correcting code (Shor 1995)

Surface code:
2D array of qubits; logical qubit encoded in boundary conditions
Threshold error rate: ~1% per gate for realistic noise models
Current hardware approaching threshold (Google, IBM, Quantinuum)
Resource overhead: ~1000 physical qubits per logical qubit at useful error rates

Threshold theorem:
If physical error rate p < p_threshold, logical error rate → 0 as code size increases.
p_threshold ≈ 1% for surface code with independent, identically distributed errors

### 13. Quantum Algorithms (#algorithms)
Deutsch-Jozsa: Determines if f:{0,1}ⁿ→{0,1} is constant or balanced in ONE query (classical: 2^(n-1)+1 queries)

Grover's search: Finds marked item in unsorted database of N elements in O(√N) queries
Amplitude amplification: (1/√N)·Σ|x⟩ → |target⟩ after ~π√N/4 iterations

Shor's factoring: Factors N-bit integer in O((log N)³) steps (classical: exponential)
Key step: quantum period finding via QFT
Breaks RSA-2048 with ~4000 logical qubits (millions of physical)

VQE (Variational Quantum Eigensolver):
Parameterized circuit U(θ)|0⟩ = |ψ(θ)⟩
Minimize ⟨ψ(θ)|H|ψ(θ)⟩ via classical optimizer
NISQ-era algorithm for quantum chemistry (LiH, BeH₂, etc.)

### 14. Analog Quantum Simulation (#analog-sim)
Bose-Hubbard model (optical lattice):
H = −J Σ_{⟨i,j⟩} (a†ᵢaⱼ + h.c.) + (U/2) Σᵢ nᵢ(nᵢ−1) − μΣᵢ nᵢ
Superfluid-Mott insulator quantum phase transition at U/J ~ 5.8z (z = coordination number)
First demonstrated: Greiner et al., Nature 2002

Transverse-field Ising model:
H = −J Σ σᵢᶻσⱼᶻ − h Σ σᵢˣ
Quantum phase transition at J/h = 1
Simulated with Rydberg atoms (QuEra, Harvard, etc.)

BEC-BCS crossover:
Fermionic atoms near Feshbach resonance interpolate between BCS (weak attraction) and BEC (tightly bound pairs)
Controlled by magnetic field near Feshbach resonance

---

## OTHER TOOLS

Sub-features embedded inside other tool pages (not standalone pages):

### Clebsch-Gordan Coefficients (`pages/lab-calculators.html`)
⟨j₁m₁;j₂m₂|JM⟩ via Wigner 3j:
= (−1)^(j₁−j₂+M)·√(2J+1)·(j₁ j₂ J; m₁ m₂ −M)
Selection rules: M = m₁ + m₂, |j₁−j₂| ≤ J ≤ j₁+j₂, triangle inequality

### PDH Locking (`pages/laser-locking.html`)
PDH error signal derivation, reflection coefficient R(ν), cavity pole ω_c = δν_cav/2,
Feedback loop bandwidth, servo bump, integrator windup.

---

## DESIGN SYSTEM TOKENS

### Colors
```
--bg-base:    #02080f    --bg-surface: #060e1c    --bg-card: #0c1526
--text-primary: #e2e8f0  --text-secondary: #94a3b8  --text-muted: #475569
--c-atom: #38bdf8   --c-lab:  #34d399   --c-qc:   #a78bfa
--c-cool: #60a5fa   --c-rr:   #fb923c   --c-calc: #fbbf24
--c-cg:   #f472b6   --c-lock: #f87171   --c-zern: #2dd4bf
```

### Typography
Inter — body text; JetBrains Mono (--font-mono) — equations and code displays

### Key CSS Components
- `.formula-box` — KaTeX math container (dark background, colored border per page)
- `.accordion` + `.accordion-header` + `.accordion-body` — collapsible sections
- `.tab-bar[data-group]` + `.tab-btn` + `.tab-panel` — tabbed content
- `.page-wrap` + `.page-hero` + `.page-content` — page layout skeleton
- `.tool-card` with `--card-color` and `--card-bg` CSS vars — home tool grid
- `.anim-in.delay-N` — scroll-reveal animation (IntersectionObserver)
